title: mdtoc slugger candidate B: unicodedata category-based slugify
description: Owns projects/mdtoc/candidates/slugger_b.py. Implement slugify(text, seen) classifying chars via unicodedata.category (keep letters/numbers/underscore, spaces to hyphens). Conform to vectors.py. Self-check with vectors.run_against, record passed/total. Stdlib only.
acceptance_criteria: Owns EXACTLY: projects/mdtoc/candidates/slugger_b.py. Touch no other file. Depends on T-023 (vectors.py) being done.
Implements slugify(text: str, seen: dict) -> str conforming to the interface documented in projects/mdtoc/candidates/vectors.py.

APPROACH = UNICODEDATA CATEGORY-BASED: classify each char with unicodedata.category (keep L* letters and N* numbers, keep underscore and hyphen, map whitespace to hyphen, drop the rest) rather than one big regex.
Self-check: import vectors and run vectors.run_against(slugify); record the exact passed/total (and any failures) in the task handoff note.
Method must be genuinely different from candidates A (regex) and C (github-slugger port).
stdlib only; no import of the mdtoc package or the other candidates.

SHARED CONVENTIONS (all mdtoc tasks): Python 3.9+ stdlib ONLY, no pip deps. Package dir projects/mdtoc/mdtoc/ (namespace package; __init__.py is created only by T-028, so test files must add projects/mdtoc/ to sys.path and import as `from mdtoc.parser import ...`). Tests use unittest and must pass via `python3 -m unittest discover -s projects/mdtoc/tests -t projects/mdtoc`. No file listed here may be created/edited by any other task (disjoint ownership).

notes.note: plan: category-based slugify via unicodedata.category (keep L*/N* + Mn/Mc/Me marks + literal _/-, map whitespace to hyphen, drop rest); dedup via seen-dict per vectors.py contract

EXPECTED: unicodedata category-based char-by-char classification (keep L*/N* + Mn/Mc/Me marks + literal _/-, map whitespace->hyphen, drop rest) would satisfy vectors.py contract incl. double-hyphen ('Foo & Bar'->'foo--bar'), CJK/accents, emoji strip, dedup counter semantics. ACTUAL: self-check 'python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'\''projects/mdtoc/candidates'\''); import vectors, slugger_b; p,t,f=vectors.run_against(slugger_b.slugify); print(f\'{p}/{t}\'); sys.exit(0 if p==t else 1)"' -> 24/24 PASS, 0 failures, on goal_mode iteration 1/3 (no fix cycles needed).

HANDOFF -> verifier: Replay: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'projects/mdtoc/candidates'); import vectors, slugger_b; p,t,f=vectors.run_against(slugger_b.slugify); print(f'{p}/{t}'); [print('FAIL',c,'exp=',e,'got=',g) for c,e,g in f]" -> observed output '24/24' with zero FAIL lines (all SLUG_VECTORS + all DEDUP_SEQUENCES pass). Approach: unicodedata.category()-based char-by-char classification (no regex) -- keep category[0] in L/N, keep Mn/Mc/Me marks, keep literal '_'/'-', map ch.isspace() to '-', drop everything else; dedup via seen-dict per vectors.py contract (base->prior-use-count, Nth repeat -> base-(N-1)). Owns exactly projects/mdtoc/candidates/slugger_b.py, stdlib-only (unicodedata), imports no mdtoc package and no sibling candidate. Did not view slugger_a.py or slugger_c.py content during implementation.

VERIFIED (conformance): fresh python3 -c vectors.run_against(slugger_b.slugify) -> passed=24 total=24, zero failures. Interface: inspect.signature = slugify(text: str, seen: dict) -> str; mutation probe -> 'foo'/'foo-1'/'foo-2', seen['foo'] bumped 1->2->3 in the SAME dict, exact per vectors.py docstring. Method-authenticity: module-level import is ONLY unicodedata; core filter is unicodedata.category(ch)[0] in ('L','N') + Mn/Mc/Me + ch.isspace(), NO re anywhere -> genuinely category-based, no core regex (its 'import vectors' is inside __main__ self-check only, not the slugify path). Independence: no sibling/mdtoc import; edge probe: B maps tab->hyphen via isspace() ('tab-here') and keeps combining marks, genuinely distinct from A (regex, strips marks) and C (rule-table, tab dropped).
